title: Add a new "test-style" Makefile target
body: I am not certain how the various `test-`\* commands (with `test-local` to come) are best to be exposed and used, but at least having `Makefile` targets is better than fishing out the commands from my shell history.

comments.body:
Oops, _now_ I have noticed `make validate`.

I’d still prefer having a `Makefile` target which runs locally without a Docker container and `sudo`, but this might need to be reworked to avoid this duplication.

`make test-lint` does warn about more things than `hack/make.sh validate-lint`, so this work at least has not been completely useless.

Anyway, I suppose this is now more of a tracked RFE than a mergeable PR.
Btw something is wrong with the shell scripts cause it isn't caching what you fixed here. Will investigate.
@mtrmac can you edit this PR and leave just the golint fixes? I'll merge it afterwards
@mtrmac I've included lint errors fixes in #15, feel free to either fix this PR or create an issue to track what we discussed here

@runcom Thanks; I have dropped the `Makefile` changes (in favor of #19), and I will close this after #15 is merged just because that way it is easier for me to verify that the changes have been included.
